title: 怪屋
summary:
Brenda Leonidis recounts her history with her late husband, Aristide, to the narrator. She describes him as a clever man who provided her with a home and happiness after they met at the Three-Leaf restaurant while she was working as a servant. She explains that she eventually discovered he was the wealthy "Leonidis Mr." and they married in a small London church. Brenda denies killing him, expressing sadness over his death and noting that family members, including Roger and Philip, have treated her poorly. She also mentions that Miss de Harvillan and the rest of the family remained in the house despite her marriage to Aristide.

Regarding the other inhabitants, Brenda describes Lawrence Brown as a sensitive and fragile man whom she tries to encourage, but she expresses fear toward Josephine, whom she finds odd and sneaky. When the narrator suggests that Brenda and Lawrence might be conspiring to poison Aristide for romantic reasons, Brenda vehemently denies any such relationship.

Later, the narrator speaks with Sophia, who remains unsympathetic toward Brenda, viewing her as manipulative. Sophia asserts that the family has no obligation to be kind to Brenda and claims that "Grandfather" was not deceived by her. According to Sophia, Grandfather had orchestrated the marriage and the hiring of Lawrence Brown as a tutor to provide Brenda with a controlled, "melancholic" romance, thereby preventing her from engaging in outside scandals.
